rss.title: More US Marines and warships to be moved to Middle East, reports say
rss.snippet: The reinforcements will come from an amphibious ready group that is usually based in Japan, an official told CBS, the BBC's US partner.

rss.title: Los Angeles asks for investigation into LA28 Olympics chief over Epstein ties
rss.snippet: The LA city council voted unanimously for an investigation into LA28 chief Casey Wasserman over his Epstein ties.

rss.title: Family of Michigan synagogue suspect killed in Lebanon airstrike, officials say
rss.snippet: The FBI said the suspect had large quantities of fireworks and petrol in his vehicle when it caught fire after ramming a synagogue on Thursday.

arxiv_papers.title: The propensity for disobedience: Rule-breaking, compliance and social phase transitions
arxiv_papers.snippet: We develop a mathematical model to describe the persistence of rule-breaking behaviors in societies, such as traffic violations, disregard for legal restrictions and other forms of noncompliance. Using a replicator-type dynamics with utility functions incorporating individual benefits, institutional

arxiv_papers.title: Social Knowledge for Cross-Domain User Preference Modeling
arxiv_papers.snippet: We demonstrate that user preferences can be represented and predicted across topical domains using large-scale social modeling. podcast: [Don't hate the replicator, hate the game] The world of science has been stuck in an existential crisis over whether we actually know the things we thought we knew. Re-running an old study today doesn't always yield the same result. Same with re-enacting old experiments.
